ai-di.titleEn: 矮 vs 低 : bas / peu élevé
ai-di.summaryEn: Two words often translated as 'bas': 矮 for concrete height, 低 for level, position, or intensity.
ai-di.htmlEn:
General Idea

Both can be translated as 'bas / peu élevé', but they don't apply to the same types of 'height'.

矮 (ǎi) : small / not very high (concrete vertical height)

  • Refers to a person's height: 个子矮.
  • Applies to vertical objects: tree, building, chair (height), etc.
  • Natural opposite: 高.
他个子不高，有点儿矮。
Tā gèzi bù gāo, yǒudiǎnr ǎi.
Il n'est pas grand, il est un peu petit.
这把椅子比较矮。
Zhè bǎ yǐzi bǐjiào ǎi.
Cette chaise est plutôt basse (en hauteur).

低 (dī) : low (level, position, intensity)

  • Low position: 低头, 低声.
  • Low level: price, salary, temperature, grade, mood.
  • Can also mean 'lower' depending on context.
请你说得低一点儿。
Qǐng nǐ shuō de dī yìdiǎnr.
Parle un peu moins fort (plus bas), s'il te plaît.
最近这个产品的价格很低。
Zuìjìn zhège chǎnpǐn de jiàgé hěn dī.
Dernièrement, le prix de ce produit est très bas.

Common Mistakes

  • Person's small height -> rather 矮 (not 低).
  • Voice/prices/levels -> rather 低 (not 矮).

an-anzhao-genju.titleEn: 按 vs 按照 vs 根据
an-anzhao-genju.summaryEn: Three similar structures to say 'according to / based on': 按 to follow a rule, 按照 to follow a rule with emphasis, 根据 to base on a base or data.
an-anzhao-genju.htmlEn:
General Idea

These three forms introduce a complement of the type 'according to / based on'. The nuance depends on the type of reference: procedure/rule vs base/evidence.

按 (àn) : according to / following

  • Very common with 按时, 按规定, 按顺序.
  • Often: follow a method, plan, order.
请按顺序排队。
Qǐng àn shùnxù páiduì.
Fais la queue dans l'ordre, s'il te plaît.
我们按计划出发。
Wǒmen àn jìhuà chūfā.
Nous partons selon le plan.

按照 (ànzhào) : according to / following

  • Similar to 按, more explicit and sometimes more formal.
  • Frequent in written instructions and rules.
请按照说明书操作。
Qǐng ànzhào shuōmíngshū cāozuò.
Suis le mode d'emploi pour effectuer l'opération.

根据 (gēnjù) : based on / according to

  • Emphasis on the base: experience, observation, documents, data.
  • Frequent with 根据经验, 根据数据, 根据你的描述.
根据数据，我们需要调整方案。
Gēnjù shùjù, wǒmen xūyào tiáozhěng fāng'àn.
D'après les données, nous devons ajuster le plan.

Quick Choices

  • Procedure / order / plan -> 按 (or 按照 for emphasis).
  • Base / evidence / data / experience -> 根据.

ban-yiban.titleEn: 半 vs 一半 : demi / moitié
ban-yiban.summaryEn: The two mean 'half', but 半 often functions as a numeral (demi-) while 一半 functions more as a noun (the half).
ban-yiban.htmlEn:
Idée générale

Les deux signifient « moitié », mais 半 se comporte souvent comme un élément numéral (demi-), alors que 一半 fonctionne davantage comme un nom.

半 (bàn) : demi- (numéral / déterminant)

  • Structure fréquente : 半 + classificateur + nom.
  • Heure : 两点半 = 2h30 (forme figée).
  • Peut apparaître dans des adjectifs : 半熟, 半新.
我等了半个小时。
Wǒ děng le bàn gè xiǎoshí.
J'ai attendu une demi-heure.
现在两点半。
Xiànzài liǎng diǎn bàn.
Il est deux heures et demie.

一半 (yíbàn) : une moitié / la moitié (nom)

  • Peut être sujet ou objet.
  • Souvent avec 的 : 一半的人 (la moitié des gens).
这份工作我只做了一半。
Zhè fèn gōngzuò wǒ zhǐ zuò le yíbàn.
Je n'ai fait que la moitié de ce travail.

Comparer vite

  • 半个苹果 = une demi-pomme (quantité).
  • 一个苹果的一半 = la moitié d'une pomme (focus sur la moitié).

Pièges

  • À l'oral, on dit parfois 半小时, mais 半个小时 est plus complet.
  • 两点半 est figé: pas de classificateur.

bao-dai.titleEn: 包 bāo et 袋 dài
bao-dai.htmlEn:
1. [cite_start]包 (bāo) : Refers to an object that envelops or a closed, structured container (bag, package, suitcase)[cite: 3].
2. [cite_start]袋 (dài) : Refers to a flexible container, often open or used for bulk storage (pouch, plastic bag, rice bag)[cite: 3].

Usage distinctions

    [cite_start]
  • 书包 (shūbāo) : The schoolbag (envelops the books)[cite: 3].
    • [cite_start]
  • 口袋 (kǒudài) : The pocket of the clothing (flexible container)[cite: 3].
  • 塑料袋 (sùliàodài) : The plastic bag.
  • 面包 (miànbāo) : The bread (as the dough envelops the air or the crumb).

As specific examples:

    [cite_start]
  • yī bāo táng : A sugar packet (closed, industrial)[cite: 3].
    • [cite_start]
  • yī dài mǐ : A rice bag (often of large size or flexible)[cite: 3].

bi.summaryEn: The comparison structure in Chinese is very different from the French 'plus... que'.
bi.htmlEn:
The comparison structure in Chinese is very different from the French 'plus... que'.

A + 比 + B + Adjective

Example: 哥哥比我高。
Gēge bǐ wǒ gāo.
(My older brother is taller than me).

Points to note:

  • Never put an intensity adverb like hěn (very) before the adjective in a structure with bǐ.
  • To say 'much more', add 得多 (de duō) after the adjective: 他比我高得多。 (Tā bǐ wǒ gāo de duō.)
  • To say 'even more', add 更 (gèng) before the adjective: 今天比昨天更冷。 (Jīntiān bǐ zuótiān gèng lěng.)
  • To say 'not as... as', often use 没有 (méiyǒu): 我没有他高。 (Wǒ méiyǒu tā gāo.)
  • To say 'as... as', often use 跟...一样 (gēn... yíyàng): 我跟他一样高。 (Wǒ gēn tā yíyàng gāo.)

bian-ci.titleEn: 遍 biàn et 次 cì
bian-ci.summaryEn: These two specific verbs translate the idea of 'times', but with an important nuance regarding duration and completion of the action.
bian-ci.htmlEn:
Ces deux spécificatifs verbaux traduisent l'idée de "fois", mais avec une nuance importante sur la durée et l'achèvement de l'action.

1. 次 (cì) : C'est le terme général pour compter les occurrences. Il indique simplement le nombre de fois qu'une action a eu lieu.

我去过两次北京。 (wǒ qù guo liǎng cì Běijīng.) - Je suis allé deux fois à Pékin.

2. 遍 (biàn) : Indique que l'action est effectuée du début à la fin, à travers tout son processus.

请再说一遍。 (qǐng zài shuō yī biàn.) - Répétez encore une fois (en entier).

这本书我看过三遍。 (zhè běn shū wǒ kàn guo sān biàn.) - J'ai lu ce livre trois fois (de la première à la dernière page).

Comparaison

Si vous dites 看了两次 (kàn le liǎng cì), vous avez ouvert le livre deux fois. Si vous dites 看了两遍 (kàn le liǎng biàn), vous l'avez lu intégralement deux fois.

bian-gai-huan.titleEn: 变 biàn , 改 gǎi , 改变 gǎibiàn
bian-gai-huan.summaryEn: Understand the usage nuances around 变 biàn , 改 gǎi , 改变 gǎibiàn.
bian-gai-huan.htmlEn:
变 (biàn) : Indique un changement d'état, de nature ou de forme. Souvent traduit par "devenir".
Ex: 天气变冷了。 (tiānqì biàn lěng le.) (Le temps est devenu froid / a changé).
改 (gǎi) : Indique une modification délibérée, une correction ou une rectification.
Ex: 改作业 (gǎi zuòyè) (corriger les devoirs), 改日子 (gǎirì zi) (changer la date prévue).
改变 (gǎibiàn) : Terme plus général et souvent plus abstrait. Il peut s'agir de changer une situation, une opinion ou le monde.
Ex: 改变主意 (gǎibiàn zhǔyi) (changer d'avis), 改变现状 (gǎibiàn xiànzhuàng) (changer le statu quo).

biaoxian-xianshi-biaoshi.summaryEn: These five terms translate to 'show' or 'express', but the nuance lies in the subject's willingness.
biaoxian-xianshi-biaoshi.htmlEn:
These five terms translate to 'show' or 'express', but the nuance lies in the subject's willingness.

1. Express and Show:
Manifestation of characteristics that reveal themselves (emotions, qualities).
  • Express (biǎoshì) : Demonstrate or express an attitude (very broad).
    Ex: 表现出爱国精神 (biǎoshì chū àiguó jīngshén) (show patriotism).
  • Show (xiǎnshì) : Emphasize the revelation or effect.
    Ex: 药效日益显示出来 (yàoxiào rìyì xiǎnshì chūlái) (the medicine's effect becomes clearer).
2. State, Declare, and Express:
Voluntary and concise expression through words or actions.
  • State (biǎoshì) : Indicate or express an attitude (very broad).
    Ex: 表示欢迎 (biǎoshì huānyíng) (welcome).
  • Declare (biǎomíng) : Clearly state one's position.
    Ex: 表明立场 (biǎomíng lìchǎng) (state one's position).
  • Express (biǎodá) : Translate deep feelings, often with effort.
    Ex: 无法用语言表达 (wúfǎ yòng yǔyán biǎodá) (the words fail to express).

bie-buyao.titleEn: Do Not and Do Not Want
bie-buyao.summaryEn: These two terms serve to express the imperative negative ('don't do...').
bie-buyao.htmlEn:
These two terms serve to express the imperative negative ('don't do...').

  • Do Not (bié) : This is the contracted form of bùyào. It is extremely frequent in spoken language.
  • Do Not Want (bùyào) : Slightly more emphatic, it can also literally mean 'do not want'.

Examples:

别走！ (bié zǒu!) - Don't go!

别忘了。 (bié wàng le.) - Don't forget.

你不要客气。 (nǐ bùyào kèqi.) - Don't be too polite.

biede-ling-lingwai.summaryEn: Indefinite usage, often plural. 别的人 (biéde rén) (other people). Talk about other things (shuō biéde ba).
biede-ling-lingwai.htmlEn:
别的 (biéde)

Usage indéfini, souvent pluriel.
别的人 (biéde rén) (d'autres personnes).
说别的吧 (shuō biéde ba) (parlons d'autre chose).

另 (lìng)

S'utilise avec un nombre ou un spécificatif (souvent pour un seul objet).
另一个极端 (lìng yī ge jíduān) (l'autre extrême).

另外 (lìngwài)

Peut être adverbe ("en plus") ou conjonction ("en outre").
Ex: 另外还有一个问题 (lìngwài hái yǒu yī ge wèntí) (De plus, il y a un autre problème).

其他 (qítā)

Équivalent formel de biéde. Souvent pour désigner "le reste".
Ex: 其他的人 (qítā de rén) (les autres personnes).

bu-mei.titleEn: 不 bù et 没 méi
bu-mei.summaryEn: These are the two basic negations. One must distinguish the adverb 没 (méi), which is a negation of an action, and the verb 没有 (méiyǒu), which means 'not have'.
bu-mei.htmlEn:
Ce sont les deux négations de base. [cite_start]Il faut distinguer l'adverbe 没 (méi), négation de l'action, et le verbe 没有 (méiyǒu), "ne pas avoir"[cite: 123, 124].

Règle fondamentale : 不 (bù) s'applique à une action ou un état qui dure ou est en cours. [cite_start]没 (méi) s'applique à une action ou un état achevé (passé ou changement d'état)[cite: 127, 128].

1. État vs Changement

天气还不冷。 Tiānqì hái bù lěng. [cite_start]Il ne fait toujours pas froid (l'état continue)[cite: 132].
天气还没冷。 Tiānqì hái méi lěng. [cite_start]Il ne fait pas encore froid (le changement n'a pas eu lieu)[cite: 133].

2. Verbes modaux et volonté

[cite_start]

Pour les verbes indiquant la volonté (vouloir, pouvoir, devoir), on utilise généralement 不, même pour le passé, car ils désignent des états durables[cite: 135, 138].

过去不允许这样做。 Guòqù bù yǔnxǔ zhèyàng zuò. [cite_start]Autrefois, il n'était pas permis d'agir ainsi[cite: 137].

3. Comparaison négative

    [cite_start]
  • 不比 (bù bǐ) : Signifie "pas plus que" (égalité ou infériorité)[cite: 142, 144].
    • [cite_start]
  • 没有/没 (méiyǒu) : Équivaut à bù rú (pas aussi... que), marquant une infériorité nette[cite: 143, 144].

4. Négations spécifiques

[cite_start]

别 (bié) : Exprime la défense (équivalent de bùyào)[cite: 147].
[cite_start]未 (wèi) : Négation formelle ou classique, utilisée à l'écrit (ex: wèihūn, célibataire)[cite: 147].

bushi-jiushi.summaryEn: Used in a context of substitution. Equates to 'either... or...'.
bushi-jiushi.htmlEn:
1. 不是……就是…… (búshi... jiùshì...)

S'emploie dans un contexte de substitution. [cite_start]Équivaut à "soit... soit..."[cite: 148, 149].

Exemple : 晚上她不是在宿舍就是在图书馆。 (wǎnshang tā bù shì zài sùshè jiùshì zài túshūguǎn.)
[cite_start]Le soir, elle est soit au dortoir, soit à la bibliothèque[cite: 151, 155].

2. 不是……而是…… (bú shì... ér shì...)

S'emploie dans un contexte d'opposition. [cite_start]Équivaut à "ce n'est pas... mais..."[cite: 156].

Exemple : 不是路太远, 而是他一点也不想来。 (bù shì lù tài yuǎn, ér shì tā yī diǎn yě bù xiǎng lái.)
[cite_start]Ce n'est pas que ce soit trop loin, c'est qu'il n'a pas du tout envie de venir[cite: 158, 159].

buzai-zaibu.titleEn: No Longer vs Not Any Longer
buzai-zaibu.summaryEn: Both mean 'ne... more...', but differ in their subjective nuance.
buzai-zaibu.htmlEn:
[cite_start]

Both mean 'ne... more...', but differ in their subjective nuance[cite: 164].

No Longer (bú zài)

Neutral and broad. [cite_start]Simple observation[cite: 165].

[cite_start]

他不再迟到了。 (tā bùzài chídào le.)
He is no longer late[cite: 167].

Not Any Longer (zài bù)

Strong and subjective. [cite_start]Often implies 'never again'[cite: 166].

[cite_start]

他再不迟到了。 (tā zài bù chídào le.)
He is no longer ever late[cite: 167].

Technical Points:

    [cite_start]
  • No Longer can be modified by intensity adverbs (e.g., jué, absolutely) [cite: 170-176].
    • [cite_start]
  • 再也不 (zài yě bù) is a very common reinforced form meaning 'never again'[cite: 171, 174].

canguan-fangwen.summaryEn: Visit (cānguān) : Visit a place (tourism, museums, campus).
canguan-fangwen.htmlEn:
[cite_start]

Visit (cānguān) : Visit a place (tourism, museums, campus)[cite: 177, 1013].

[cite_start]

Access (făngwèn) : Visit people (official visit, interview, meeting of experts)[cite: 177, 1014].

Comparative Examples:

    [cite_start]
  • Visit Peking University (cānguān Běijīng dàxué) : Visit the campus/grounds of the university[cite: 1017, 1026].
    • [cite_start]
  • Access Peking University (fǎngwèn Běijīng dàxué) : Make an official visit to authorities or professors[cite: 1018, 1027].

Other Forms of Visits:

    [cite_start]
  • Visit (bàifăng) : Respectful visit to an elder or authority[cite: 1038].
    • [cite_start]
  • Visit (kàn) / Visit (kànwàng) : Visit friends or family (informal)[cite: 1041, 1043].
    • [cite_start]
  • Visit (tàn wàng) : Visit involving a long journey (e.g., returning home)[cite: 1041, 1047].

changchang-wangwang.titleEn: Frequency: Often: Often vs Often: Usually
changchang-wangwang.summaryEn: Understand the usage nuances around Often: Often vs Often: Usually.
changchang-wangwang.htmlEn:
Often (chángcháng) : Indicates pure frequency. [cite_start]Can be used in all contexts (past, present, future)[cite: 188, 1104].
Usually (wăngwăng) : Indicates regularity based on experience. [cite_start]Often requires a specific context or condition[cite: 188, 1073].

Major Differences:

    [cite_start]
  • Future: One cannot use wăngwăng for future actions (e.g., "They will often ski this winter" uses only chángcháng)[cite: 191, 1103].
    • [cite_start]
  • Negation: One can say bù cháng (not often), but never *bù wăng[cite: 191, 1104].

chuan-dai.titleEn: Wearing: To Wear (穿) vs To Wear (戴)
chuan-dai.summaryEn: The choice depends on the object that is 'put on' or 'hung on'.
chuan-dai.htmlEn:
The choice depends on the object that is 'put on' or 'hung on'[cite: 179, 1130].

[cite_start] [cite_start] [cite_start] [cite_start]
VerbUsageExamples
Wear (穿)Main clothes, shoes, pants (idea of passing through/enfolding)[cite: 1130].穿衣服、穿鞋子、穿裙子 (chuān yīfu、chuān xié zi、chuān qúnzi) [cite: 1131]
Wear (戴)Accessories, hats, jewelry, glasses, watches, gloves[cite: 1133, 1134].戴帽子、戴眼镜、戴手表、戴手套 (dài màozi、dài yǎnjìng、dài shǒubiǎo、dài shǒutào) [cite: 1134, 1138]

Special Cases of the Verb 'Wear' (穿 and 戴):

    [cite_start]
  • Carry (背) : Carry on the back (bag, child)[cite: 181, 1162].
    • [cite_start]
  • Hold (抱) : Carry in arms[cite: 181, 1167].
    • [cite_start]
  • Hold (提) : Carry by hand (suitcase)[cite: 181, 1158].
    • [cite_start]
  • Open Umbrella (撑) : Carry an open umbrella[cite: 181, 1152].

ci-bian.titleEn: Compter les occurrences : 次, 遍, 回, 趟
ci-bian.summaryEn: 次 (cì) : The general term. Indicates repetition without specifying the duration.
ci-bian.htmlEn:
• 次 (cì) : The general term. [cite_start] Indicates repetition without specifying the duration [cite: 182, 1180].
  • 遍 (biàn) : Indicates that the action is done entirely, from start to finish (e.g., reading a book entirely) [cite: 182, 1202].
  • 回 (huí) : Synonymous with cì, more colloquial or dialectal [cite: 186, 1198].
  • 趟 (tàng) : Specific to journeys (round trip) [cite: 186, 1217].

Multiplication: 倍 (bèi) and 乘 (chéng)

  • 倍 (bèi) : "Times more". [cite_start] Used in everyday language (e.g., liǎng bèi, double) [cite: 187, 1220].
  • 乘 (chéng) : "Multiply by". [cite_start] Formal mathematical term [cite: 187, 1220].

da.summaryEn: The word 打 (dǎ) has many uses, grouped here by main sense categories.
da.htmlEn:
• 1. Strike, fight, destroy:
    • 打孩子 (dǎ háizi) : beat the child
    • 打架 (dǎjià) : fight
    • 打字 (dǎ zì) : type
    • 碗打了 (wǎn dǎ le) : the bowl is broken
  • 2. Play a game:
    • 打球 (dǎ qiú) : play ball
    • 打牌 (dǎpái) : play cards
  • 3. Make, sew, erect:
    • 打墙 (dǎ qiáng) : build a wall
    • 打毛线 (dǎ máo xiàn) : knit
    • 打领带 (dǎ lǐngdài) : tie a tie
  • 4. Fetch, buy, pack:
    • 打水 (dǎ shuǐ) : fetch water
    • 打酱油 (dǎ jiàngyóu) : buy soy sauce
    • 打行李 (dǎ xíngli) : pack one's luggage
  • 5. Trigger, emission, contact:
    • 打电话 (dǎ diànhuà) : make a phone call
    • 打招呼 (dǎ zhāohu) : greet
    • 打针 (dǎzhēn) : get a shot
  • 6. Unintentional body movements:
    • 打哈欠 (dǎ hāqian) : yawn
    • 打呼噜 (dǎ hūlu) : snore
    • 打喷嚏 (dǎ pēntì) : sneeze

dang-dang-shi.summaryEn: 是 (shì) exprime une identité essentielle ou permanente. 当 (dāng) implique souvent un changement d'état ou une situation provisoire/professionnelle.
dang-dang-shi.htmlEn:
1. Prononciation dāng (1er ton) :
    [cite_start]
  • Face à : 当着我的面 (dāngzhe wǒ de miàn) (en ma présence)[cite: 1185].
    • [cite_start]
  • Assumer / Faire face : 当家 (dāng jiā) (diriger le ménage), 当心 (dāngxīn) (faire attention)[cite: 1187, 1188].
    • [cite_start]
  • Profession : 当经理 (dāng jīnglǐ) (être directeur), 当翻译 (dāng fānyì) (être interprète)[cite: 1192, 1193].
    • [cite_start]
  • Temps : 当时 (dāngshí) (à ce moment-là), 当中 (dāngzhōng) (au milieu de)[cite: 1198].
2. Prononciation dàng (4e ton) :
    [cite_start]
  • Approprié : 恰当 (qiàdàng) (approprié), 用词不当 (yòng cí bù dāng) (mots mal choisis)[cite: 1201].
    • [cite_start]
  • Considérer comme / Croire : 当成朋友 (dàngchéng péngyou) (considérer comme ami), 你当真吗? (nǐ dàngzhēn ma?) (tu es sérieux ?)[cite: 1205, 1208].
    • [cite_start]
  • Immédiat : 当天 (dàngtiān) (le jour même), 当时 (dāngshí) (immédiatement)[cite: 1209].

Comparaison : dāng vs 是 (shì)

是 (shì) exprime une identité essentielle ou permanente.
[cite_start]当 (dāng) implique souvent un changement d'état ou une situation provisoire/professionnelle[cite: 1216, 1217].

Exemple : 他当过经理。 (tā dāng guo jīnglǐ.) (Il a été directeur - action passée achevée). [cite_start]On ne peut pas dire *shìguo[cite: 1223].

Comparaison : dāng vs 成为 (chéngwéi)

[cite_start]

成为 (chéngwéi) insiste sur le résultat du changement d'état (devenir)[cite: 1230, 1231].

作为 (zuòwéi) vs 当做 (dāngzuò)

[cite_start]

En tant que préposition ("en tant que"), seul 作为 est possible[cite: 1249, 1250].
[cite_start]Exemple : 作为一个人... (zuòwéi yī gèrén...) (En tant qu'être humain...)[cite: 1251].

dong-mingbai-lijie.titleEn: 懂 dǒng , 明白 míngbai , 理解 lǐjiě
dong-mingbai-lijie.summaryEn: All three mean 'understand', but with different usage nuances.
dong-mingbai-lijie.htmlEn:
[cite_start]

All three mean 'understand', but with different usage nuances[cite: 1299].

懂 (dǒng) : To grasp the meaning or have knowledge/skill.
[cite_start]懂法语 (dǒng Fǎyǔ) (know French), 懂技术 (dǒng jìshù) (be good at technology)[cite: 1312].
明白 (míngbai) : To fully grasp the reality of something. [cite_start]Also means 'clear'[cite: 1314, 1318].
[cite_start]说得更明白些 (shuō de gèng míngbai xiē) (express yourself more clearly)[cite: 1320].
理解 (lǐjiě) : Intellectual or rational comprehension. [cite_start]It can be modified by a degree adverb[cite: 1306, 1307].
[cite_start]非常理解 (fēicháng lǐjiě) (comprehend perfectly)[cite: 1309].
[cite_start]

Note technical : dǒng and míngbai can be resultative verbs (ex: 听得懂 (tīng de dǒng)), while lǐjiě is always the main verb[cite: 1303].

dou-quan.titleEn: 都 dōu et 全 quán
dou-quan.summaryEn: Understand the usage nuances around 都 dōu et 全 quán.
dou-quan.htmlEn:
Usage of 都 (dōu)

1. [cite_start]Totalizing what precedes : 他们都来了 (tāmen dōu lái le) (They are all here)[cite: 1325, 1336].
2. [cite_start]With "连" (Even) : 连我都不知道 (lián wǒ dōu bùzhī dào) (Even I don't know)[cite: 1361, 1364].
3. [cite_start]Indicating 'Already' : 今天都星期五了 (jīntiān dōu xīngqī wǔ le) (It's already Friday)[cite: 1367, 1368].
4. [cite_start]Blame it on... : 都是你不好 (dōu shì nǐ bù hǎo) (It's your fault)[cite: 1365, 1366].

Comparison with 全 (quán)

    [cite_start]
  • Substitution : quán can replace dōu to totalize people or indicate fault[cite: 1373].
    • [cite_start]
  • Incompatibility : quán does not use with quantity words like hěnduō or měi[cite: 1379].
    • [cite_start]
  • Adjective : Only quán means 'whole' or 'complete' before a noun (ex: 全世界 (quán shìjiè) - the whole world)[cite: 1385, 1387].

er-liang.summaryEn: Although both mean 'two', their usage is governed by precise rules of numbering and quantity.
er-liang.htmlEn:
[cite_start]

Although both mean 'two', their usage is governed by precise rules of numbering and quantity[cite: 1426].

1. E (èr) : Used for counting and numbering (cardinal and ordinal numbers, fractions) [cite: 1427-1428].
[cite_start] [cite_start] [cite_start] [cite_start]
一, 二, 三1, 2, 3 [cite: 1430]
二十二22 [cite: 1430]
二楼1st floor (literally 2nd level) [cite: 1430]
百分之二2% [cite: 1430]
2. Liǎng : Used to indicate quantity before a specific number [cite: 1428]. Often implies a dual or couple idea [cite: 1430-1431].
[cite_start] [cite_start] [cite_start]
两本书Two books [cite: 1430]
两汉The Han dynasty (literally 2nd Han) [cite: 1431]
两口子A couple [cite: 1431]

Special Cases

    [cite_start]
  • Composite Numbers: Use èr in all composite numbers (e.g., 22), but liǎng is more common before 百 (hundred), 千 (thousand), 万 (ten thousand) to indicate the quantity of hundreds or thousands [cite: 1439-1440].
    • [cite_start]
  • Never Use Liǎng Before 十 (shí): Always say èrshí (20) [cite: 1442-1445].
    • [cite_start]
  • Measurement Specifics: Before jīn (pound), gōngjīn (kilo), or mǐ (meter), both are possible: 二斤 / 两斤 (èr jīn / liǎng jīn) [cite: 1466-1467].

gangcai-gang.titleEn: 刚才 gāngcái et 刚 gāng
gangcai-gang.summaryEn: These two words indicate that an action is very recent, but their grammatical nature differs.
gangcai-gang.htmlEn:
These two words indicate that an action is very recent, but their grammatical nature differs.

刚才 (gāngcái) : Time noun
  • Means "just now".
  • Can be placed before or after the subject.
  • One can add the negation bù to it.
  • 刚才你去哪儿了？ (gāngcái nǐ qù nǎr le?) (Where were you just now?)
刚 (gāng) : Adverb
  • Means "just".
  • Always goes right before the verb (after the subject).
  • Cannot be followed by a negation.
  • 他刚走。 (tā gāng zǒu.) (He just left.)

gongzuo-laodong.titleEn: Travailler : 工作, 劳动, 干活, 做事
gongzuo-laodong.summaryEn: These verbs are not interchangeable and depend on the nature of the work.
gongzuo-laodong.htmlEn:
工作 (gōngzuò) : Professional or remunerated work (an "employment").
找不到工作 (zhǎo bù dào gōngzuò) (Can't find work).
劳动 (láodòng) : Physical, manual work, or noble human activity.
劳动人民 (láodòng rénmín) (Working class).
干活 (gànhuó) : Concrete activity, often small physical tasks.
她整天干活 (tā zhěngtiān gān huó) (She works all day/always busy).
做事 (zuòshì) : More vague term meaning "take care of something" or "have a position".
她在医院做事 (tā zài yīyuàn zuò shì) (She has a position in a hospital).

guan-fuze-sai.titleEn: S'occuper de : 管, 负责, 带, 照顾, 关心
guan-fuze-sai.summaryEn: Understand the usage nuances around S'occuper de : 管, 负责, 带, 照顾, 关心.
guan-fuze-sai.htmlEn:
[cite_start]管 (guǎn) : Implies control, supervision, or authority [cite: 1829-1830].
[cite_start]管孩子 (guǎn háizi) (look after/educate children) [cite: 1831-1832].
[cite_start]带 (dài) : Physically take care of a child (the "look after") [cite: 1840].
[cite_start]请人来家里带孩子 (qǐng rén lái jiā lǐ dài háizi) (Ask someone to come and look after the children) [cite: 1838-1839].
[cite_start]照顾 (zhàogù) : Take care of dependent persons (the sick, the elderly) [cite: 1846-1847].
[cite_start]照顾病人 (zhàogù bìngrén) (look after a patient) [cite: 1848, 1849].
[cite_start]负责 (fùzé) : Be responsible for an official or professional task [cite: 1854].
[cite_start]这事由你负责 (zhè shì yóu nǐ fùzé) (It is your responsibility) [cite: 1856-1857].
[cite_start]关心 (guānxīn) : Be concerned about, show interest or psychological attention [cite: 1861].
[cite_start]非常关心国家大事 (fēicháng guānxīn guójiā dàshì) (Be very concerned about national affairs) [cite: 1863-1864].

hai-ye-you-zai.titleEn: Encore et Aussi : 还, 也, 又, 再
hai-ye-you-zai.summaryEn: Understand the usage nuances around 也 (yě), 还 (hái), 再 (zài), and 又 (yòu).
hai-ye-you-zai.htmlEn:
也 (yě) : Also / Also. Always placed before the verb.
[cite_start]我也去。
Wǒ yě qù. (I also go).

还 (hái) : Still / Also / Moreover. Indicates continuity or adds information.
[cite_start]他还在睡觉。
Tā hái zài shuìjiào. (He is still sleeping).

再 (zài) : Again (future). For a repetition to come or be desired.
[cite_start]明天再说吧。
Míngtiān zài shuō ba. (We'll talk about it later).

又 (yòu) : Again (already realized). For a repetition already realized.
[cite_start]今天他又迟到了。
Jīntiān tā yòu chídào le. (He is still late today).

Key Nuance Point: 又 vs 再

昨天他又迟到了。
Zuótiān tā yòu chídào le. (He was late again yesterday).

明天别再迟到了。
Míngtiān bié zài chídào le. (Don't be late again tomorrow).

haishi-houzhe.titleEn: Still or Either: háishi and huòzhě
haishi-houzhe.summaryEn: In French, the word 'or' is versatile. In Chinese, you need to choose based on whether the sentence is a question or an affirmation.
haishi-houzhe.htmlEn:
En français, le mot "ou" est polyvalent. En chinois, il faut choisir selon que la phrase est une question ou une affirmation.

1. 还是 (háishi) : S'utilise exclusivement dans les phrases interrogatives (choix entre deux options).
你喝咖啡还是喝茶？ (nǐ hē kāfēi háishi hē chá?)
(Nǐ hē kāfēi háishi hē chá?) - Bois-tu du café ou du thé ?
2. 或者 (huòzhě) : S'utilise dans les phrases affirmatives.
我喝咖啡或者茶都可以。 (wǒ hē kāfēi huòzhě chá dōu kěyǐ.)
(Wǒ hē kāfēi huòzhě chá dōu kěyǐ.) - Je peux boire du café ou du thé.

Cas particulier :

On peut trouver háishi dans une phrase qui semble affirmative mais qui contient une interrogation indirecte :
我不知道他去还是不去。 (wǒ bùzhī dào tā qù háishi bù qù.) (Je ne sais pas s'il y va ou non).

he.summaryEn: The word 'and' (hé) is closest to the 'and' in French, but its usage is much more restricted.
he.htmlEn:
[cite_start]

Le mot 和 (hé) est le plus proche du "et" français, mais son usage est beaucoup plus restreint [cite: 1470-1471].

[cite_start]Règle d'or : 和 ne relie en principe que des groupes nominaux (noms, pronoms)[cite: 1473, 1477].

1. Liaison de noms

[cite_start]

我和你 (wǒ hé nǐ) (toi et moi) [cite: 1543-1544].
[cite_start]北京、上海和广州 (Běijīng、shàng hǎi hé guǎng zhōu) (Pékin, Shanghai et Canton)[cite: 1544].
[cite_start]Note : On utilise la "semi-virgule" (顿号 dùnhào) entre les premiers termes d'une énumération [cite: 1550-1551].

2. Ce que 和 ne fait pas

Il ne relie généralement pas de verbes ou d'adjectifs. Pour cela, le chinois utilise :

    [cite_start]
  • Une simple virgule : 干净, 很整齐 (gānjìng, hěn zhěngqí) (propre et bien rangé) [cite: 1553-1554].
    • [cite_start]
  • Des adverbes : 又快又好 (yòu kuài yòu hǎo) (vite et bien) [cite: 1556-1557].
    • [cite_start]
  • La conjonction 而且 (érqiě) : Pour relier des propositions [cite: 1618-1619].

3. Usage prépositionnel ("avec")

[cite_start]

和 signifie souvent "avec" ou "à" [cite: 1543-1544].
[cite_start]我和他商量一下 (wǒ hé tā shāngliang yī xià) (J'en discute avec lui) [cite: 1723-1724].

hen-henduo-duole.summaryEn: The word 'beaucoup' is not always translated as 多 (duō). The translation depends on the grammatical function.
hen-henduo-duole.htmlEn:
The word 'beaucoup' is not always translated as 多 (duō). [cite_start]The translation depends on the grammatical function[cite: 3].

1. Adverb before a sentiment verb: 很 (hěn)
In Chinese, 'beaucoup' is not used before sentiment verbs, but 'very' is used.
[cite_start]Example: I like beaucoup la Chine → I like very China. (Je aime very China.)[cite: 3].
2. Adjective (large quantity): 很多 (hěn duō)
[cite_start]Example: There are beaucoup people → There are many people. (Il y a beaucoup de gens.)[cite: 3].
3. 'Beaucoup plus' (comparison): 得多 (de duō)
Example: It is beaucoup more expensive → It is much more expensive. (C'est beaucoup plus cher.)[cite: 3].
4. 'Beaucoup' of actions: 多 (duō)
Used before the verb to advise doing something more.
[cite_start]Example: Drink beaucoup water → Drink more water. (Bois beaucoup d'eau.)[cite: 3].

hui-neng-keyi.summaryEn: Note: To say 'is it possible?', 能 and 可以 are often interchangeable.
hui-neng-keyi.htmlEn:
会 (huì) : Acquired ability through learning.
我会说汉语。 (wǒ huì shuō Hànyǔ.) (I know how to speak Chinese).
能 (néng) : Physical ability, aptitude, or material possibility.
我不舒服，不能去。 (wǒ bù shūfu, bù néng qù.) (I am not comfortable, I cannot go).
可以 (kěyǐ) : Permission, authorization, or general possibility.
这儿可以抽烟吗？ (zhèr kěyǐ chōuyān ma?) (Is it allowed to smoke here?).

Note: To say 'is it possible?', 能 and 可以 are often interchangeable.

jiang-hui-yao.summaryEn: Mandarin does not have a future tense. Instead, adverbs or modal verbs are used to indicate an upcoming action.
jiang-hui-yao.htmlEn:
Mandarin does not have a future tense. [cite_start]Instead, adverbs or modal verbs are used to indicate an upcoming action. [cite: 1723-1727].

1. [cite_start]将 (jiāng) / 将要 (jiāngyào) : Upcoming action, especially in writing [cite: 1745-1746].
[cite_start]火车将进站了 (huǒchē jiāng jìn zhàn le) (The train is about to arrive at the station) [cite: 1749, 1750].
2. [cite_start]会 (huì) : Indicates probability or certainty [cite: 1785-1786].
[cite_start]我想他不会来 (wǒ xiǎng tā bù huì lái) (I think he won't come) [cite: 1788-1789].
3. [cite_start]要 (yào) : Indicates intention or imminence [cite: 1745, 1768].
[cite_start]要下雨 (yào xià yǔ) (It's going to rain) [cite: 1775-1777].
4. [cite_start]快要……了 (kuàiyào... le) : Immediate future ("on the verge of")[cite: 1746, 1768].
[cite_start]春节快要到了 (Chūnjié kuàiyào dào le) (It's going to be soon the Chinese New Year) [cite: 1757, 1758].

jie-huan.titleEn: Borrowing and Returning (借 and 还)
jie-huan.summaryEn: A particularity of Mandarin is that the verb 借 covers both the opposite meanings of 'borrow' and 'lend' alone.
jie-huan.htmlEn:
A particularity of Mandarin is that the verb 借 covers both the opposite meanings of 'borrow' and 'lend' alone.

1. 借 (jiè) : Lend

Often used with 给 (give).
我跟图书馆借了一本书。 (wǒ gēn túshūguǎn jiè le yī běn shū.) (I borrowed a book from the library).

2. 借 (jiè) / 借给 (jiègěi) : Borrow

Often used with 给 (give) to avoid ambiguity.
他借给我一百块钱。 (tā jiè gěi wǒ yī bǎi kuài qián.) (He lent me 100 yuan).

3. 还 (huán) : Return / Repay

Be careful with pronunciation (do not confuse with the adverb 还, meaning 'still').
别忘了还书。 (bié wàng le hái shū.) (Don't forget to return the book).

jiu-cai.titleEn: 就 jiù vs 才 cái
jiu-cai.summaryEn: These two adverbs are among the most complex for French speakers as they express the subjective perception of time or quantity by the speaker.
jiu-cai.htmlEn:
These two adverbs are among the most complex for French speakers as they express the subjective perception of time or quantity by the speaker.

Adverb Temporal sense Quantitative sense
就 (jiù) Action done quickly, easily, soon or soon.
他八点就来了。 (tā bā diǎn jiù lái le.) (He was already there at 8h).		 Indicates that it is a lot or only (depending on the context).
就要五块钱。 (jiùyào wǔ kuài qián.) (It costs only 5 yuan).
才 (cái) Action done slowly, hardly, unwillingly.
他八点才来。 (tā bā diǎn cái lái.) (He only came at 8h).		 Indicates that it is little or only now.
他才十岁。 (tā cái shí suì.) (He is only 10 years old).

juede-yiwei-renwei.titleEn: Penser : 觉得, 以为, 认为
juede-yiwei-renwei.summaryEn: juéde: Expresses a personal sentiment, impression, or subjective opinion. Ex: 我觉得这件衣服很漂亮。 (wǒ juéde zhè jiàn yīfu hěn piàoliang.) (I think this piece of clothing is pretty).
juede-yiwei-renwei.htmlEn:
觉得 (juéde) : Expresses a personal sentiment, impression, or subjective opinion.
Ex: 我觉得这件衣服很漂亮。 (wǒ juéde zhè jiàn yīfu hěn piàoliang.) (I think this piece of clothing is pretty).

以为 (yǐwéi) : "Believe wrongly". Often used when one has mistaken a situation.
Ex: 我以为你不在家。 (wǒ yǐwéi nǐ bù zàijiā.) (I thought you were not at home).

认为 (rènwéi) : More formal. Expresses a reflective judgment, conviction, or argued opinion.
Ex: 我不认为这是个好主意。 (wǒ bù rènwéi zhè shì ge hǎo zhǔyi.) (I do not consider it a good idea).

kan-jian.titleEn: Look at kàn vs See jiàn
kan-jian.summaryEn: The distinction is between the process (to look at) and the result (to see).
kan-jian.htmlEn:
The distinction is between the process (to look at) and the result (to see).

Look at (kàn) : Look / Read

Indicates the voluntary action of directing one's gaze or reading.
Look at the book (kàn shū) (read a book), Look at the movie (kàn diànyǐng) (watch a movie).

See (jiàn) : See / Notice

Indicates the result of visual perception.
Notice (kànjiàn) (notice), Hear (tīngjiàn) (hear).

The resultative verb See (kànjiàn) :

This is the most common form to translate "see". Kàn is the action, jiàn is the success of the action.

lai-qu.summaryEn: The choice depends solely on the interlocutor's position (central deixis).
lai-qu.htmlEn:
The choice depends solely on the interlocutor's position (central deixis).

Come (lái) : Movement towards the interlocutor (here).
You come to me here. (nǐ dào wǒ zhèr lái.) (Come to me).
Go (qù) : Movement away from the interlocutor (there).
I go to the store. (wǒ qù shāngdiàn.) (I go to the store).

As directional suffixes :

Placed after an action verb, they indicate the direction of the action :
Come in (jìnlái) (come in here) vs Come in (jìnqù) (come in there).

le.summaryEn: It is not a tense marker (past), but an aspect or change marker.
le.htmlEn:
It is not a tense marker (past), but an aspect or change marker.

  • 了 (le) of change (at the end of the sentence): Indicates that a new situation appears.
  • 我不去了。 (wǒ bù qù le.) (I won't go anymore / I've changed my mind).
  • 我买了两本书。 (wǒ mǎi le liǎng běn shū.) (I bought two books).

mai-mai.summaryEn: These two words are distinguished only by tone at oral level, which is a major difficulty.
mai-mai.htmlEn:
These two words are distinguished only by tone at oral level, which is a major difficulty.

  • 买 (mǎi) : 3rd tone (descending-up) = Buy.
  • 卖 (mài) : 4th tone (even and descending) = Sell.

Mnemonic trick: The character for "sell" (卖) has an additional element above that of "buy" (买). One can say that one needs to "possess something more" to be able to sell it.

Combined example: 买卖 (mǎimài) means "commerce" or "business".

shi-you.titleEn: is vs have : être et avoir
shi-you.summaryEn: Two fundamental verbs often confused: is expresses identity (être), while have expresses possession (avoir) or existence.
shi-you.htmlEn:
Overview

Mot Pinyin Sens Négation
is is être (identité) is not
have have avoir / il y a do not have

is (is) - Being / Equality

is establishes a relationship of identity or equality between the subject and what follows.

Uses

  1. Identify someone or something

He is a teacher. Tā shì lǎoshī. Il est professeur.

This is my book. Zhè shì wǒ de shū. C'est mon livre.

  1. Confirm or affirm

Yes, is. Duì, shì de. Oui, c'est ça.

Negation of is

Important : Always use not with is, never no !

He is not a student. Tā bù shì xuéshēng. Il n'est pas étudiant.

have (have) - Having / There is

have expresses possession or existence.

Uses

  1. Ownership

I have a book. Wǒ yǒu yì běn shū. J'ai un livre.

She has two children. Tā yǒu liǎng gè háizi. Elle a deux enfants.

  1. Existence (there is)

There is a cup of coffee on the table. Zhuōzi shàng yǒu yì bēi kāfēi. Il y a une tasse de café sur la table.

There are many students in the classroom. Jiàoshì lǐ yǒu hěn duō xuéshēng. Il y a beaucoup d'étudiants dans la salle de classe.

Negation of have

Important : Always use no with have, never not !

I do not have money. Wǒ méiyǒu qián. Je n'ai pas d'argent.

There is no one here. Zhèlǐ méiyǒu rén. Il n'y a personne ici.

Direct comparison

French With is With have
C'est un livre There is is a book ✗
I have a book ✗ There is have a book
He is a teacher There is is a teacher ✗
He has a teacher ✗ There is have a teacher

Common errors

Error 1: Using is to describe a place

Incorrect Correct
There is a book on the table There is have a book

To say "there is X somewhere", use have.

Error 2: Using have to identify

Incorrect Correct
This is my phone There is is my phone

To say "this is X", use is.

Error 3: Incorrect negation

Incorrect Correct
Not is is not
No have do not have

Mnemonic trick

  • is = "c'est" (identity, equality)
  • have = "there is" (possession, existence)

Ponder: "C'est = is" and "There is = have"

Exercises

Complete with is or have:

  1. I ___ a Chinese person.
  2. You ___ a phone?
  3. This ___ what?
  4. In the room, there ___ three people.
See answers 1. is 2. have 3. is 4. have

ting-tingzhe.summaryEn: To say stop in Chinese: stop is often used for stop/pause (vehicle, service), stop is more explicit for ceasing an activity.
ting-tingzhe.htmlEn:
Overview

In Chinese, 'stop' often means stop/pause or stop (cessation of an activity). The right choice depends on what you are stopping.

1) Stop a vehicle / park: stop (停车)

  • Park/stop the vehicle: 停车.
  • Stop: 停下来.
Please park here.
Qǐng zài zhèlǐ tíngchē.
Garde-toi ici, s'il te plaît.
The car stopped.
Chē tíng xiàlái le.
La voiture s'est arrêtée.

2) Stop doing something: stop + V / don't + V

  • stop + V : cease (more explicit and often more formal).
  • don't + V : stop doing... (direct request / prohibition).
Please stop talking.
Qǐng tíngzhǐ shuōhuà.
Please stop talking.
Don't argue, listen to me first.
Bié chǎo le, xiān tīng wǒ shuō.
Don't argue, listen to me first.

3) Stop of a service: power outage / water cut-off

  • Power outage: 停电, water cut-off: 停水.
  • Common structure: ...停了 (it's cut off / it stopped).
Last night, the power was cut off.
Zuówǎn tíngdiàn le.
Last night, there was a power outage.

Common pitfalls

  • To a vehicle, avoid stop car -> prefer parking.
  • For a formal activity, stop is often more natural than stop alone.

wen-yao-yaoqiu.summaryEn: Keep Chinese characters, pinyin, markdown symbols and HTML tags unchanged whenever they already exist.
wen-yao-yaoqiu.htmlEn:
[cite_start]

It is important to distinguish three main senses in French:[/cite_start]

1. Interrogate / Ask a question: 问 (wèn) [cite_start]

她没问我的意见。 (tā méi wèn wǒ de yìjiàn.) (She didn't ask my opinion.)[/cite: 1268].

2. Request / Demand:
  • Simple Request: 要 (yào). [cite_start]Ex: 要水 (yào shuǐ) (Request water)[cite: 1273].
  • Formal Request: 申请 (shēnqǐng). [cite_start]Ex: 申请工作 (shēnqǐng gōngzuò) (Apply for a job)[cite: 1273].
  • Strong Request: 要求 (yāoqiú). [cite_start]Ex: 要求和平 (yāoqiú hépíng) (Request peace)[cite: 1273].
    • [cite_start]
  • Request to do: 请 (qǐng) or 让 (ràng)[cite: 1276].
3. Need / Require: 需要 (xūyào) or 要 (yào) [cite_start]

旅行需要三个小时。 (lǚxíng xūyào sān ge xiǎoshí.) (The trip requires three hours)[cite: 1287].

Self-questioning:

[cite_start]

One uses 考虑 (kǎolǜ) to reflect, or 想知道 (xiǎng zhīdào) for curiosity[cite: 1296].

xiang-yao.titleEn: 想 xiǎng et 要 yào
xiang-yao.summaryEn: These two verbs translate to 'want,' but with different degrees of intensity and intention.
xiang-yao.htmlEn:
These two verbs translate to 'want,' but with different degrees of intensity and intention.

想 (xiǎng) : The desire / The envy

This is a desire, a reflection, often less categorical.
我想学中文。 (wǒ xiǎng xué Zhōngwén.) (I would like / I intend to learn Chinese).

Other meanings: Think, reflect, or miss someone (我想你 (wǒ xiǎng nǐ) : you make me miss you).

要 (yào) : The will / The decision

This is a clear will, a decision made, or a necessity.
我要学中文。 (wǒ yào xué Zhōngwén.) (I want / I am going to learn Chinese - it's decided).

Negation: To politely refuse an offer, one generally uses 不想 (bù xiǎng) rather than 不要 (bùyào) (which can seem abrupt, except for saying 'don't do that').

yidian-youdian.titleEn: Un peu : 一点儿 vs 有点儿
yidian-youdian.summaryEn: The classic mistake is to interchange them. Their position relative to the adjective is the key.
yidian-youdian.htmlEn:
The classic mistake is to interchange them. Their position relative to the adjective is the key.

有点儿 (yǒudiǎnr) + Adj

Se places before the adjective. Often expresses a sense of embarrassment or a negative opinion.
今天有点儿冷。
Jīntiān yǒudiǎnr lěng.
(It's a bit too cold today).
这件衣服有点儿贵。
Zhè jiàn yīfu yǒudiǎnr guì.
(This garment is a bit expensive).

Adj + 一点儿 (yīdiǎnr)

Se places after the adjective. Used for comparison or to request a change.
请快一点儿。
Qǐng kuài yīdiǎnr.
(Please, go a bit faster).
便宜一点儿吧。
Piányi yīdiǎnr ba.
(A bit cheaper, please).

Point nuance

For a quantity ("a bit of water"), generally uses 一点儿 + noun: 我想喝一点儿水。 (Wǒ xiǎng hē yīdiǎnr shuǐ.)

yiqian-yihou.titleEn: yǐqián et yǐhòu : Before and After
yiqian-yihou.summaryEn: These words come after the temporal reference or action: Temporal/Action + yǐqián/yǐhòu.
yiqian-yihou.htmlEn:
Ces mots se placent après le repère temporel ou l'action : Temps/Action + 以前/以后.

1. En tant que prépositions :

吃饭以前
Chīfàn yǐqián.
(Avant de manger / Avant le repas).

三天以后
Sān tiān yǐhòu.
(Dans trois jours / Après trois jours).

到家以后给我打电话。
Dào jiā yǐhòu gěi wǒ dǎ diànhuà.
(Après être rentré(e), appelle-moi).

2. En tant qu'adverbes :

以前, 我不认识他。
Yǐqián, wǒ bù rènshi tā.
(Auparavant, je ne le connaissais pas).

以后, 请你准时。
Yǐhòu, qǐng nǐ zhǔnshí.
(À l'avenir, s'il te plaît, sois à l'heure).

Point nuance

以前 peut parler du passé ("auparavant"), alors que 以后 peut parler de la suite ("plus tard", "à l'avenir").

zhe-na.titleEn: zhè and nà : This and That
zhe-na.summaryEn: The demonstrative Chinese words zhè (this, near) and nà (that, far) with their compound forms zhège and zhèr.
zhe-na.htmlEn:
Vue d'ensemble

Mot Pinyin Sens Distance
这 zhè ce, ceci Proche du locuteur
那 nà ce, cela Éloigné du locuteur

这 (zhè) - Ceci / Ce (proche)

这 désigne quelque chose de proche du locuteur.

Exemples de base

这是我的书。 Zhè shì wǒ de shū. C'est mon livre. (que je tiens ou qui est près de moi)

这很好吃。 Zhè hěn hǎochī. C'est délicieux. (ce que je mange)

Formes composées

Forme Pinyin Sens
这个 zhège celui-ci, cette chose-ci
这里/这儿 zhèlǐ/zhèr ici
这些 zhèxiē ceux-ci (pluriel)

这个苹果很甜。 Zhège píngguǒ hěn tián. Cette pomme est sucrée.

我住在这里。 Wǒ zhù zài zhèlǐ. J'habite ici.

那 (nà) - Cela / Ce (loin)

那 désigne quelque chose d'éloigné du locuteur.

Exemples de base

那是什么？ Nà shì shénme? C'est quoi (là-bas) ?

那很贵。 Nà hěn guì. C'est cher. (quelque chose au loin)

Formes composées

Forme Pinyin Sens
那个 nàge celui-là, cette chose-là
那里/那儿 nàlǐ/nàr là-bas
那些 nàxiē ceux-là (pluriel)

那个人是谁？ Nàge rén shì shéi? Qui est cette personne (là-bas) ?

他在那里工作。 Tā zài nàlǐ gōngzuò. Il travaille là-bas.

Comparaison visuelle

[Moi] ←这 (proche)→ . . . . . . ←那 (loin)→ [Là-bas]

这里 = ici (où je suis)
那里 = là-bas (ailleurs)

Utilisation avec 个 (classificateur)

En pratique, on utilise souvent 这个/那个 plutôt que 这/那 seuls :

这个好。 (plus naturel que 这好) Zhège hǎo. Celui-ci est bien.

我要那个。 Wǒ yào nàge. Je veux celui-là.

Expressions courantes

Expression Pinyin Sens
这样 zhèyàng comme ceci, ainsi
那样 nàyàng comme cela
这么 zhème si, tellement (intensité proche)
那么 nàme alors, si, tellement

为什么这么贵？ Wèishénme zhème guì? Pourquoi c'est si cher ?

那么，我们走吧。 Nàme, wǒmen zǒu ba. Alors, allons-y.

哪 (nǎ) - Lequel ? (interrogatif)

À ne pas confondre avec 那 ! 哪 (nǎ) est la forme interrogative :

Mot Pinyin Sens
哪 nǎ quel, lequel
哪个 nǎge lequel
哪里/哪儿 nǎlǐ/nǎr où

你要哪个？ Nǐ yào nǎge? Tu veux lequel ?

你住在哪里？ Nǐ zhù zài nǎlǐ? Tu habites où ?

Tableau récapitulatif

Proche Loin Question
这 zhè 那 nà 哪 nǎ
这个 那个 哪个
这里 那里 哪里
这些 那些 哪些
这样 那样 怎样

Exercices

Complétez avec 这, 那 ou 哪 :

  1. ___ 是我妈妈。(C'est ma mère - montrant une photo que je tiens)
  2. ___ 座山很高。(Cette montagne est haute - au loin)
  3. 你要 ___ 个？(Tu veux lequel ?)
  4. 我喜欢 ___ 里。(J'aime bien ici)
Voir les réponses 1. 这 (proche) 2. 那 (loin) 3. 哪 (question) 4. 这 (ici = proche)

zhidao-renshi.titleEn: 知道 zhīdào et 认识 rènshi
zhidao-renshi.summaryEn: Used for facts, news, addresses, or abstract data. 你知道他的名字吗？ (nǐ zhīdào tā de míngzi ma?) (Sais-tu son nom ?)
zhidao-renshi.htmlEn:
1. 知道 (zhīdào) : Know an information

Used for facts, news, addresses, or abstract data.
你知道他的名字吗？ (nǐ zhīdào tā de míngzi ma?) (Sais-tu son nom ?)

2. 认识 (rènshi) : Know a person, a Chinese character, or a place one has physically practiced.

Used for people, Chinese characters, or places one has physically practiced.
我不认识他。 (wǒ bù rènshi tā.) (Je ne le connais pas).
你认识这个汉字吗？ (nǐ rènshi zhè ge Hànzì ma?) (Reconnais-tu ce caractère ?)

Note : For the "know-how" (e.g., know how to swim), we use 会 (huì), as seen previously.

bang-bangzhu-bangmang.summaryEn: These three terms mean 'to help', but differ in their grammatical nature and register.
bang-bangzhu-bangmang.htmlEn:
[cite_start]

Ces trois termes signifient "aider", mais diffèrent par leur nature grammaticale et leur registre[cite: 3].

1. 帮 (bāng) : Principalement utilisé à l'oral. [cite_start]C'est un verbe simple qui peut être suivi d'un objet direct (la personne que l'on aide)[cite: 3].
2. 帮助 (bāngzhù) : Plus formel que bāng. [cite_start]Il peut fonctionner comme verbe ou comme nom ("une aide")[cite: 3].
3. 帮忙 (bāngmáng) : C'est un verbe sécable (verbe-objet). [cite_start]On ne peut pas dire "帮忙 + quelqu'un"[cite: 3].

Exemples comparatifs

你能帮我吗？Peux-tu m'aider ? (Courant)
他经常帮助我。Il m'aide souvent. (Plus formel)
请帮我的忙。S'il te plaît, aide-moi. (Structure correcte pour bāngmáng)
谢谢你的帮助。Merci pour ton aide. (Bāngzhù utilisé comme nom)
[cite_start]

Note : Avec bāngmáng, si l'on veut préciser la personne aidée, on utilise la structure : 帮 + Personne + (的) + 忙[cite: 3].

bien.summaryEn: In Chinese, the word 'bien' is extremely versatile and changes its translation based on the context.
bien.htmlEn:
En chinois, le mot "bien" est extrêmement polyvalent et change de traduction selon le sens.

1. Qualité / Performance : 好 (hǎo)
Ex: 他画得很好 (tā huà de hěn hǎo) (Il dessine très bien).
2. Santé / Confort : 好 (hǎo)
Ex: 病人今天好了 (bìngrén jīntiān hǎo le) (Le malade va bien aujourd'hui).
3. Justesse / Correction : 对 (duì)
Ex: 这样做很对 (zhèyàng zuò hěn duì) (Tu as bien agi / c'est correct).
4. Quantité ("Bien des...") : 很多 (hěnduō)
Ex: 很多年以来 (hěnduō nián yǐlái) (depuis bien des années).
5. Intensité ("Bien froid") : 很 (hěn) ou 好 (hǎo)
Ex: 今天好冷啊 ! (jīntiān hǎo lěng a!) (Il fait vraiment froid aujourd'hui !).

de-de-de.titleEn: Structural Particles: 的, 地, 得
de-de-de.summaryEn: Tip: If a word connects to a noun, it's often 的 ; if it connects to a verb with its degree/result complement, it's 得.
de-de-de.htmlEn:
的 (de) : Determiner + 的 + Noun.
Indicates possession or qualification of an object.
Ex: 我的书
Wǒ de shū. (My book).
Ex: 漂亮的衣服
Piàoliang de yīfu. (Beautiful clothes).
地 (de) : Adjective + 地 + Verb.
Forms an adverb (equivalent of "-ment").
Ex: 慢慢地走
Mànmàn de zǒu. (Walk slowly).
Ex: 认真地学习
Rènzhēn de xuéxí. (Study seriously).
得 (de) : Verb + 得 + Adjective.
Indicates the degree, appreciation, or result of an action.
Ex: 她说得很好
Tā shuō de hěn hǎo. (She speaks very well).
Ex: 他跑得很快
Tā pǎo de hěn kuài. (He runs very fast).

Tip: If a word connects to a noun, it's often 的 ; if it connects to a verb with its degree/result complement, it's 得.

faire.summaryEn: 'Faire' is a versatile word with many meanings that can be translated in various ways in Chinese.
faire.htmlEn:
[cite_start]

"Faire" est un mot-outil aux sens multiples qui se traduit de façons très diverses en chinois [cite: 1573-1575].

1. Fabriquer / Confectionner : 做 (zuò)
[cite_start]做蛋糕 (zuò dàngāo) (faire un gâteau), 做衣服 (zuò yīfu) (faire des vêtements)[cite: 1841, 1842].
2. Produire / Émettre :
[cite_start]生孩子 (shēng háizi) (faire un enfant) [cite: 1845, 1846][cite_start], 发烧 (fāshāo) (faire de la température)[cite: 1849, 1850].
3. Effectuer / Pratiquer :
[cite_start]学习 (xuéxí) (faire des études) [cite: 1852, 1853][cite_start], 打网球 (dǎ wǎngqiú) (faire du tennis)[cite: 1855, 1856].
4. Faire office de / Paraître :
[cite_start]装傻 (zhuāng shǎ) (faire l'idiot) [cite: 1623-1624][cite_start], 显得年轻 (xiǎnde niánqīng) (faire jeune)[cite: 1638].
5. Impersonnel (Météo) : 天气 (tiānqì)
[cite_start]今天很热 (jīntiān hěn rè) (Il fait chaud aujourd'hui)[cite: 1680].

xian-xianshi-biaoshi.summaryEn: Understand the usage nuances around Be / Change / Swap / Change / Change.
xian-xianshi-biaoshi.htmlEn:
XiaoLearn · Fiche lexicale
Become vs modify vs swap vs change(s) vs change (impact) — Back to index
General Idea
  • Be biàn : become / transform (state change, often intransitive).
  • Change gǎi : modify / correct (intentional, transitive).
  • Swap huàn : replace / exchange (substitution A↔B).
  • Change(s) biànhuà : change(s) (often noun: “there are changes”).
  • Change (impact) gǎibiàn : change (with impact), more neutral / written style.

1) Be (biàn) : become

Structures

  • Be + ADJ : become + adjective.
  • 变得 + ADJ : emphasizes the degree of change.
  • 变成 + N : “transform into …”.
天气变冷了。
Tiānqì biàn lěng le.
Le temps est devenu froid.
他变得更自信了。
Tā biàn de gèng zìxìn le.
Il est devenu plus confiant.
水变成冰了。
Shuǐ biànchéng bīng le.
L'eau s'est transformée en glace.

Pitfalls

  • To say “correct a text / change a schedule”, Change is often more appropriate.

2) Change (gǎi) : modify / correct

Goal

  • Change by modifying (text, plan, habit, setting) ; often “intentional”.

Structures

  • Change + N : modify N.
  • 改成 + N : change to become N (chosen new version).
  • 把 + N + 改… : very frequent structure.
这句话你改一下。
Zhè jù huà nǐ gǎi yíxià.
Modifie un peu cette phrase.
我们改成线上开会吧。
Wǒmen gǎichéng xiànshàng kāihuì ba.
Passons plutôt en réunion en ligne.

Exception

  • Change also serves for “correct” in the sense of “rectify an error”.

3) Swap (huàn) : replace / exchange

Goal

  • Replace A by B, change of concrete object, exchange of money, etc.

Structures

  • Swap + N : change of N (clothing, place…).
  • 换成 + N : replace by N.
我想换一个座位。
Wǒ xiǎng huàn yí gè zuòwèi.
Je voudrais changer de place.
可以把现金换成欧元吗？
Kěyǐ bǎ xiànjīn huànchéng ōuyuán ma?
On peut changer l'argent en euros ?

Pitfalls

  • “Modify a text” → Change, not Swap (except if you replace an element by another: “replace a word”).

4) Change(s) (biànhuà) vs Change (gǎibiàn)

Change(s) : “changes” (often noun)

  • 有变化, 发生变化, 变化很大.
最近安排有变化。
Zuìjìn ānpái yǒu biànhuà.
Récemment il y a eu des changements d'organisation.

Change (impact) : “change (something)” (often verb, more “impact”)

  • 改变计划/态度/决定 ; sometimes intransitive: 情况改变了.
这个决定改变了很多事情。
Zhège juédìng gǎibiàn le hěn duō shìqing.
Cette décision a changé beaucoup de choses.

Checklist

  • Be → Be
  • Modify/correct → Change
  • Replace/exchange → Swap
  • Changes (noun) → Change(s)
  • Change with impact → Change (impact)
